topStories.description: Former US President Barack Obama on Friday urged Virginia voters to vote “Yes” on a redistricting referendum as early voting continues ahead of the April 21 special election. The measure would let the state legislature temporarily redraw congressional maps

topStories.title: Fifteen South American people deported from the US arrive in DR Congo
topStories.description: The deportees from South America are the first to be sent to the DRC in a deal agreed between Washington and Kinshasa

topStories.title: Inside Kharg Island, Iran’s oil lifeline under threat
topStories.description: With the US blockading Iran’s ports, Kharg Island is in focus. How are civilians caught in the middle?

topStories.title: Trump Says US, Iran "Very Close" To Having Peace Deal
topStories.description: Trump's comments came after a series of social media posts in which he touted progress on reopening the Strait of Hormuz and ending Iran's nuclear program.

dailyNews.title: Officer on horseback chases suspect through New York streets
dailyNews.description: The suspect was accused of purse-snatching and has been charged with larceny and providing false information.

dailyNews.title: US Congress temporarily extends controversial surveillance power under FISA
dailyNews.description: The measure has long been criticised for allowing US intelligence agencies to collect citizen data without a warrant.

dailyNews.title: World reacts to Strait of Hormuz reopening amid US-Iran conflict
dailyNews.description: Politicians and industry welcome reopening of the vital waterway after throttling of traffic caused oil prices to spike.

dailyNews.title: US confirms transit fare spike to $150 for World Cup fans in New Jersey
dailyNews.description: Fares confirmed at nearly 12-time hike to get from Manhattan’s Penn Station to the Metlife Stadium in East Rutherford.

dailyNews.title: Scholar Rumeysa Ozturk returns to Turkiye following Trump deportation push
dailyNews.description: Ozturk says she made the decision to avoid 'losing more time to the state-imposed violence and hostility' under Trump.

dailyNews.title: "Mine Threat In Parts Of Hormuz Not Fully Understood": US Navy Advisory
dailyNews.description: The TSS refers to the so-called Traffic Separation Scheme, which was adopted by the UN's shipping agency in 1968 with agreement of countries in the region.

dailyNews.title: Widow, 86, back in France after being held by ICE
dailyNews.description: Marie-Thérèse had moved to the US after reuniting with her long-lost love, an American man who died in January.

dailyNews.title: US Will Not Release Frozen Iranian Funds, says Trump; Peace Talks Likely On Weekend
dailyNews.description: US president says most points in a potential Iran deal have been finalised, with talks likely over the weekend; Tehran has yet to confirm the claims

dailyNews.title: Traders place $760 million bet on falling oil ahead of Hormuz announcement
dailyNews.description: Just ahead of pivotal updates concerning the Strait of Hormuz and Iran, lucrative oil trades totaling millions were executed, raising eyebrows among U.S. officials. Such timely trades have prompted a thorough investigation into several oil futures transactions made right before key policy changes, with the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission now scrutinizing the details of these deals.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says US And Iran Are Working Together To Clear Mines From Hormuz Strait
dailyNews.description: Last week, the New York Times reported that Iran was unable to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz because it could not locate all the mines it laid in the waterway to block the passage amid war.

dailyNews.title: ‘Insult to Nari Shakti’: Amit Shah fumes at opposition as women's reservation amendment fails in Lok Sabha
dailyNews.description: Amit Shah said Congress and allies blocked the passage of the essential constitutional amendment for the law granting 33% reservation for women in legislatures.

dailyNews.title: Trump's big claim after Iran reopens Strait of Hormuz: 'Has agreed to never close it'
dailyNews.description: Hours after Iran announced that the Strait of Hormuz is reopened for commercial vessels, US President Donald Trump said the Islamic Republic has agreed to never close it again.

dailyNews.title: America 250 events taking place this summer called 'once-in-a-lifetime' travel opportunities
dailyNews.description: The U.S. is marking its 250th birthday with carefully planned nationwide events, including tall ship tours, a state fair in D.C., Mount Rushmore fireworks and IndyCar races.

dailyNews.title: Maryland high school employee arrested on accusations of filming female students in dressing room
dailyNews.description: A Maryland high school employee was arrested and charged with sex abuse of a minor after allegedly filming students in a girls' changing room at school.

dailyNews.title: FIFA Host Committee, NJ Transit say plan for World Cup at MetLife Stadium will be a success amid $150 trains
dailyNews.description: The FIFA New York New Jersey Host Committee backed NJ Transit’s World Cup transportation plan despite $150 travel costs to MetLife Stadium, as officials clashed over concerns.

dailyNews.title: One million bees swarm highway after crash shuts down interstate ramp for hours
dailyNews.description: A truck carrying one million bees crashed on Interstate 40 in Knoxville, Tennessee, closing part of the highway as beekeepers scrambled to contain them.

dailyNews.title: Committee to Protect Journalists demands Kuwait release American reporter Ahmed Shihab-Eldin
dailyNews.description: The CPJ demands Kuwait release detained American journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin, who reportedly faces charges tied to broad national security laws.

dailyNews.title: Daughter of missing American in Bahamas says Brian Hooker using mother's illness as 'excuse' to leave country
dailyNews.description: Daughter of American woman missing in The Bahamas accuses stepfather Brian Hooker of using his mother's illness as an excuse to leave the country.

topStories.description: The White House says it is working with the FBI to review cases of American scientists who have gone missing or died in recent years in a growing list.

topStories.title: Democratic lawmaker rages against Trump over war, yells 'f--- his a--!'
topStories.description: Rep. Sydney Kamlager-Dove unleashes profane attack on Trump over Iran war costs, saying "f--- his a--" while criticizing daily war spending.

topStories.title: Trump Is Urged to Act on Iranian Site Feared Impervious to Airstrikes
topStories.description: Little is known about Pickaxe Mountain, but some experts say it illustrates the impossibility of relying on force alone to prevent Iran from acquiring a nuclear bomb.

topStories.title: Prosecutor Withdraws From Trump Team’s Investigation of Ex-CIA Director John O. Brennan
topStories.description: A career Justice Department lawyer, Maria Medetis Long, in Miami is said to have raised concerns about whether the evidence justified moving forward with a bid to prosecute John O. Brennan.

dailyNews.description: A career Justice Department lawyer, Maria Medetis Long, in Miami is said to have raised concerns about whether the evidence justified moving forward with a bid to prosecute John O. Brennan.

dailyNews.title: The 27-Year-Old Diplomat Waging Trump’s Cultural War With Europe
dailyNews.description: Five years out of college, Samuel Samson has driven the Trump administration’s push to upend America’s postwar relationship with Europe.

dailyNews.title: Federal Appeals Court Opens Door to Moving Trans Inmates Under Trump Gender Order
dailyNews.description: A three-judge panel gave a group of 17 transgender women a few weeks to seek further recourse in court before their transfer to men’s facilities could take effect.

dailyNews.title: Lutnick Says Canada Trade Deal Needs to Be Reworked Ahead of Talks
dailyNews.description: Howard Lutnick, President Trump’s commerce secretary, derided Canada’s trade strategy and said a North American deal needed to be reworked.

dailyNews.title: Hundreds of Fake Pro-Trump Avatars Emerge on Social Media
dailyNews.description: The artificial-intelligence-generated fake influencers have surged on TikTok, Instagram, Facebook and YouTube in an apparent bid to hook conservative voters.

dailyNews.title: Iran announces the Strait of Hormuz is open
dailyNews.description: Iran announced that the Strait of Hormuz is completely open for commercial ships for the remainder of the ceasefire. President Donald Trump also confirmed the news. NBC News' Matt Bradley reports on what this could mean for the future as peace talks with Iran could continue.

dailyNews.title: Trump expected to nominate ousted FEMA chief to lead agency again
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump is expected to nominate Cameron Hamilton to serve as administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency, nearly a year after he was removed as its acting head.

dailyNews.title: Judge halts construction on Trump's White House ballroom
dailyNews.description: Judge halts construction on Trump's White House ballroom

dailyNews.title: White House holds press briefing
dailyNews.description: Watch live coverage as White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt holds a press briefing.

dailyNews.title: Trump says U.S. will continue blockade after Iran says strait "completely open"
dailyNews.description: President Trump has welcomed Iran's announcement that, at least while the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire holds, the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" to all ships.

dailyNews.title: Trump says Iranians have "agreed to everything," including removal of enriched uranium
dailyNews.description: President Trump spoke with CBS News Friday in a new telephone interview.

dailyNews.title: U.S. could send senior officials back to Pakistan for Iran talks within days, sources say
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration is considering a renewed diplomatic push that could send officials back to Pakistan within days, sources said.

dailyNews.title: White House eyes alleged Utah scandal as GOP frets about redistricting loss
dailyNews.description: White House officials are leaning on Utah Republicans to further examine ethics concerns regarding a relationship between two key players in a key gerrymandering case, sources told CBS News.
